scenarios.title: Graph Learning Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Graph neural network tasks, graph construction, message passing, PyTorch DGL layers, sparse graph operations, and DGL training workflows.
scenarios.role: Guides DGL graph APIs, GNN model construction, message passing, sparse operations, and full-graph training workflows.
scenarios.read_when: The request names DGL, Deep Graph Library, DGLGraph, heterograph, dgl.nn, dgl.function, GraphConv, SAGEConv, GATConv, HeteroGraphConv, dgl.sparse, or graph neural network training with DGL.
scenarios.best_for: Constructing DGL graphs, debugging graph feature schemas, implementing DGL message passing and PyTorch GNN layers, and running safe smoke checks for DGL model workflows.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is about a different graph-learning framework such as PyG only, or about generic graph algorithms without DGL APIs.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `dgl` when DGL-specific graph objects, APIs, layers, or runtime errors appear; use more general graph skills only when no DGL package or API surface is involved.

scenarios.title: Graph Data Pipelines
scenarios.when_to_read: Graph dataset ingestion, CSV graph folders, DGLDataset caches, GraphBolt OnDiskDataset metadata, DGL-Go YAML configs, and graph-data validation tasks.
scenarios.role: Provides DGL-specific data layout, cache, serialization, GraphBolt metadata, and DGL-Go config validation guidance.
scenarios.read_when: The request names DGLDataset, CSVDataset, meta.yaml, DGL save_graphs/load_graphs, OnDiskDataset, GraphBolt metadata.yaml, DGL-Go, dgl configure, dgl recipe, or DGL YAML configs.
scenarios.best_for: Validating DGL CSV datasets, writing custom DGLDataset subclasses, preparing GraphBolt metadata, linting DGL-Go configs, and linking data layout to training routes.
scenarios.avoid_when: The data format is not for DGL or GraphBolt, or the task only needs generic CSV validation without graph semantics.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `dgl` for graph-specific dataset folders, DGL-Go recipes, and GraphBolt data pipelines; use generic data validation skills only before the data is mapped to DGL graph semantics.

scenarios.title: Distributed Graph Training
scenarios.when_to_read: DGL distributed graph partitioning, DistGraph/DistDataLoader training, ip_config files, partition config validation, launch command construction, and GraphBolt distributed partition notes.
scenarios.role: Supplies safe DGL distributed preflight, partition metadata checks, and dry launch-command construction without starting cluster jobs.
scenarios.read_when: The request names dgl.distributed, DistGraph, DistDataLoader, node_split, edge_split, partition_graph, part_config, ip_config.txt, DGL launch.py, graph partitions, or distributed GraphBolt.
scenarios.best_for: Checking DGL partition metadata, planning trainer script changes, constructing reviewable launch commands, and identifying when SSH/shared-storage/GPU approval is required.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is generic distributed systems work with no DGL partition or graph training surface, or when the user asks to run a cluster without confirming hosts and workspace safety.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `dgl` when distributed graph training depends on DGL partition metadata or DistGraph APIs; keep execution dry until cluster preconditions are confirmed.
